title: State transition enforcement
p3_observations: P3: precheckControlPlaneClaudeReviewStateTransitionEnforcement duplicates the already-established state-transition precheck pattern from closed Hermes and AI-handoff slices. This is accepted for this per-subphase implementation style and deferred as a future extraction candidate.
P3: normalizeOptionalStateTransitionClaim is outside the diff but exists as a pre-existing module-scope helper used by already-closed state-transition slices. No action required for S09.
review_text: Claude returned GO for RP00.P02.M04.S09 with no P0, P1, or P2 findings. Claude verified that precheckControlPlaneClaudeReviewStateTransitionEnforcement consumes and revalidates the S08 result, preserves claude_review plus claude-opus-4-8 plus max plus read-only metadata, emits a frozen metadata-only state transition receipt, rejects forbidden and unknown claims, and does not invoke runtime Claude, write review queues, write audit ledgers/events, execute runtime permission/AuthZ/permission-engine logic, execute service logic, create runtime routes, or write product state. Two P3 observations were informational only: cross-subphase duplication remains an established local pattern, and the shared transition-claim normalizer exists outside the diff.

findings.title: State transition precheck logic duplicates established closed-slice patterns.
findings.body: No action required. The duplication is the established per-subphase pattern in this package and is deferred as a future extraction candidate outside S09.

findings.title: normalizeOptionalStateTransitionClaim is outside the diff packet.
findings.body: No action required. The helper is pre-existing module-scope code used by already-closed state-transition slices and is covered by current service tests.
